moduleDescription:
This module contains a few procedures to control the terminal (also called console). On UNIX, the implementation simply uses ANSI escape sequences and does not depend on any other module, on Windows it uses the Windows API. Changing the style is permanent even after program termination! Use the code exitprocs.addExitProc(resetAttributes) to restore the defaults. Similarly, if you hide the cursor, make sure to unhide it with showCursor before quitting.

Progress bar

Basic progress bar example:

Playing with colorful and styled text

Procs like styledWriteLine, styledEcho etc. have a temporary effect on text parameters. Style parameters only affect the text parameter right after them. After being called, these procs will reset the default style of the terminal. While setBackGroundColor, setForeGroundColor etc. have a lasting influence on the terminal, you can use resetAttributes to reset the default style of the terminal.

entries.name: getCursorPos
entries.description: Returns cursor position (x, y) writes to stdout and expects the terminal to respond via stdin

entries.name: terminalWidthIoctl
entries.code:
proc terminalWidthIoctl(fds: openArray[int]): int {.raises: [], tags: [],
    forbids: [].}
entries.description: Returns terminal width from first fd that supports the ioctl.

entries.name: terminalHeightIoctl
entries.code:
proc terminalHeightIoctl(fds: openArray[int]): int {.raises: [], tags: [],
    forbids: [].}
entries.description: Returns terminal height from first fd that supports the ioctl.

entries.name: terminalWidth
entries.description: Returns some reasonable terminal width from either standard file descriptors, controlling terminal, environment variables or tradition.

entries.name: terminalHeight
entries.description: Returns some reasonable terminal height from either standard file descriptors, controlling terminal, environment variables or tradition. Zero is returned if the height could not be determined.

entries.name: terminalSize
entries.code:
proc terminalSize(): tuple[w, h: int] {.raises: [], tags: [ReadEnvEffect],
                                        forbids: [].}
entries.description: Returns the terminal width and height as a tuple. Internally calls terminalWidth and terminalHeight, so the same assumptions apply.

entries.name: hideCursor
entries.description: Hides the cursor.

entries.name: showCursor
entries.description: Shows the cursor.

entries.name: setCursorPos
entries.code:
proc setCursorPos(f: File; x, y: int) {.raises: [IOError],
                                        tags: [WriteIOEffect], forbids: [].}
entries.description: Sets the terminal's cursor to the (x,y) position. (0,0) is the upper left of the screen.

entries.name: setCursorXPos
entries.code:
proc setCursorXPos(f: File; x: int) {.raises: [IOError], tags: [WriteIOEffect],
                                      forbids: [].}
entries.description: Sets the terminal's cursor to the x position. The y position is not changed.

entries.name: cursorUp
entries.code:
proc cursorUp(f: File; count = 1) {.raises: [IOError], tags: [WriteIOEffect],
                                    forbids: [].}
entries.description: Moves the cursor up by count rows.

entries.name: cursorDown
entries.code:
proc cursorDown(f: File; count = 1) {.raises: [IOError], tags: [WriteIOEffect],
                                      forbids: [].}
entries.description: Moves the cursor down by count rows.

entries.name: cursorForward
entries.code:
proc cursorForward(f: File; count = 1) {.raises: [IOError],
    tags: [WriteIOEffect], forbids: [].}
entries.description: Moves the cursor forward by count columns.

entries.name: cursorBackward
entries.code:
proc cursorBackward(f: File; count = 1) {.raises: [IOError],
    tags: [WriteIOEffect], forbids: [].}
entries.description: Moves the cursor backward by count columns.

entries.name: eraseLine
entries.description: Erases the entire current line.

entries.name: eraseScreen
entries.description: Erases the screen with the background colour and moves the cursor to home.

entries.name: resetAttributes
entries.description: Resets all attributes.

entries.name: Style
entries.code:
Style = enum
  styleBright = 1,          ## bright text
  styleDim,                 ## dim text
  styleItalic,              ## italic (or reverse on terminals not supporting)
  styleUnderscore,          ## underscored text
  styleBlink,               ## blinking/bold text
  styleBlinkRapid,          ## rapid blinking/bold text (not widely supported)
  styleReverse,             ## reverse
  styleHidden,              ## hidden text
  styleStrikethrough         ## strikethrough
entries.description: Different styles for text output.

entries.name: ansiStyleCode
entries.code: proc ansiStyleCode(style: int): string {.raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}

entries.name: setStyle
entries.code:
proc setStyle(f: File; style: set[Style]) {.raises: [IOError],
    tags: [WriteIOEffect], forbids: [].}
entries.description: Sets the terminal style.

entries.name: writeStyled
entries.code:
proc writeStyled(txt: string; style: set[Style] = {styleBright}) {.
    raises: [IOError], tags: [WriteIOEffect], forbids: [].}
entries.description: Writes the text txt in a given style to stdout.

entries.name: ForegroundColor
entries.code:
ForegroundColor = enum
  fgBlack = 30,             ## black
  fgRed,                    ## red
  fgGreen,                  ## green
  fgYellow,                 ## yellow
  fgBlue,                   ## blue
  fgMagenta,                ## magenta
  fgCyan,                   ## cyan
  fgWhite,                  ## white
  fg8Bit,                   ## 256-color (not supported, see `enableTrueColors` instead.)
  fgDefault                  ## default terminal foreground color
entries.description: Terminal's foreground colors.

entries.name: BackgroundColor
entries.code:
BackgroundColor = enum
  bgBlack = 40,             ## black
  bgRed,                    ## red
  bgGreen,                  ## green
  bgYellow,                 ## yellow
  bgBlue,                   ## blue
  bgMagenta,                ## magenta
  bgCyan,                   ## cyan
  bgWhite,                  ## white
  bg8Bit,                   ## 256-color (not supported, see `enableTrueColors` instead.)
  bgDefault                  ## default terminal background color
entries.description: Terminal's background colors.

entries.name: setForegroundColor
entries.code:
proc setForegroundColor(f: File; fg: ForegroundColor; bright = false) {.
    raises: [IOError], tags: [WriteIOEffect], forbids: [].}
entries.description: Sets the terminal's foreground color.

entries.name: setBackgroundColor
entries.code:
proc setBackgroundColor(f: File; bg: BackgroundColor; bright = false) {.
    raises: [IOError], tags: [WriteIOEffect], forbids: [].}
entries.description: Sets the terminal's background color.

entries.name: ansiForegroundColorCode
entries.code:
proc ansiForegroundColorCode(fg: ForegroundColor; bright = false): string {.
    raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}

entries.name: ansiForegroundColorCode
entries.code:
template ansiForegroundColorCode(fg: static[ForegroundColor];
                                 bright: static[bool] = false): string

entries.name: ansiForegroundColorCode
entries.code:
proc ansiForegroundColorCode(color: Color): string {.raises: [], tags: [],
    forbids: [].}

entries.name: ansiBackgroundColorCode
entries.code:
proc ansiBackgroundColorCode(color: Color): string {.raises: [], tags: [],
    forbids: [].}

entries.name: setForegroundColor
entries.code:
proc setForegroundColor(f: File; color: Color) {.raises: [IOError],
    tags: [RootEffect, WriteIOEffect], forbids: [].}
entries.description: Sets the terminal's foreground true color.

entries.name: setBackgroundColor
entries.code:
proc setBackgroundColor(f: File; color: Color) {.raises: [IOError],
    tags: [RootEffect, WriteIOEffect], forbids: [].}
entries.description: Sets the terminal's background true color.

entries.name: isatty
entries.code: proc isatty(f: File): bool {.raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
entries.description: Returns true if f is associated with a terminal device.

entries.name: TerminalCmd
entries.code:
TerminalCmd = enum
  resetStyle,               ## reset attributes
  fgColor,                  ## set foreground's true color
  bgColor                    ## set background's true color
entries.description: commands that can be expressed as arguments

entries.name: styledWrite
entries.code: macro styledWrite(f: File; m: varargs[typed]): untyped
entries.description: Similar to write, but treating terminal style arguments specially. When some argument is Style, set[Style], ForegroundColor, BackgroundColor or TerminalCmd then it is not sent directly to f, but instead corresponding terminal style proc is called.

entries.name: styledWriteLine
entries.description: Calls styledWrite and appends a newline at the end.

entries.name: styledEcho
entries.description: Echoes styles arguments to stdout using styledWriteLine.

entries.name: getch
entries.code:
proc getch(): char {.raises: [IOError, EOFError], tags: [ReadIOEffect],
                     forbids: [].}
entries.description: Reads a single character from the terminal, blocking until it is entered. The character is not printed to the terminal.

entries.name: readPasswordFromStdin
entries.code:
proc readPasswordFromStdin(prompt: string; password: var string): bool {.
    tags: [ReadIOEffect, WriteIOEffect], raises: [IOError], forbids: [].}

entries.name: readPasswordFromStdin
entries.code:
proc readPasswordFromStdin(prompt = "password: "): string {.raises: [IOError],
    tags: [ReadIOEffect, WriteIOEffect], forbids: [].}
entries.description: Reads a password from stdin without printing it.

entries.name: resetAttributes
entries.description: Resets all attributes on stdout. It is advisable to register this as a quit proc with exitprocs.addExitProc(resetAttributes).

entries.name: isTrueColorSupported
entries.description: Returns true if a terminal supports true color.

entries.name: enableTrueColors
entries.description: Enables true color.

entries.name: disableTrueColors
entries.description: Disables true color.
